event: The Procession of Saint Gregory the Great
symbols: litany text with saints' names — the Church calling upon the communion of saints for intercession
white-robed procession — penitential prayer and supplication
fallen figure in blue — victim of the plague for which Gregory ordered the procession
medieval towers and city — Rome during the plague, the context of this penitential rite
illuminated initial letter — the liturgical and literary context of the prayer
description: An illuminated manuscript page from the Tres Riches Heures. The lower portion depicts a procession of white-robed monks or clergy walking in two columns past medieval buildings with towers and spires. A figure in ornate episcopal vestments leads from the right. On the ground at center, a figure in blue robes lies collapsed or dead. Above the miniature, two columns of Latin litany text fill most of the page, listing saints' names with 'ORA' (pray) responses. A decorated initial letter with vine scrollwork adorns the upper left corner.
